title: [LowerSeqToSV] struct_create with clock field produces invalid IR
body:
Input:
```mlir
module {
  hw.module @ClockAgg(%c: !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>) -> (oc: !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>) {
    %clock = hw.struct_extract %c["clock"] : !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>
    %0 = hw.struct_create (%clock) : !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>
    hw.output %0 : !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>
  }
}
```

`circt-opt --lower-seq-to-sv clock-agg.mlir`

```
clock-agg.mlir:4:10: error: 'hw.struct_create' op structure field `"clock"` type does not match
    %0 = hw.struct_create (%clock) : !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>
         ^
clock-agg.mlir:4:10: note: see current operation: %1 = "hw.struct_create"(%0) : (i1) -> !hw.struct<clock: !seq.clock>
```

Crafted example after encountering error "in the wild" with `-preserve-aggregate=all`.  Here's a FIRRTL snippet this is reachable from, FWIW, using `-preserve-aggregate=all -scalarize-top-module=false`:

```firrtl
circuit ClockAgg:
  module ClockAgg:
    input c: {clock: Clock}
    output oc: {clock: Clock}
    oc.clock <= asClock(asUInt(c.clock))
```

The above FIRRTL works in 1.53.0 but produces the above in 1.54.0 and on main.
